zone_name: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

689.name: Marked Path
689.description: A path skirting around the outside edge of a wide rockslide is clearly marked out with large white pieces of quartz. It meanders down the slope and out of sight behind a rocky hill, and meets another less well kept path above you which was destroyed by the massive slide.

sw.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

u.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

690.name: Stone Arch
690.description: A massive stone arch straddles the path here, one side made up of a small rocky cliff. Hidden from sight by the same cliff, until you're upon them, are a large workshop and a low house, built from slabs of quartz flecked granite. Both are nestled up against the north end of the hidden vale, while to your south and west the rock has been carved down and away into wide quarry.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

n.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

sw.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

691.name: Granite Quarry
691.description: Most of a small hill has been carved away, where it all went and how it all got there, is a question - given the treacherous path into the vale - that has no apparent answer. In some places the walls are over twenty five feet high, seemingly taken out in impossibly huge slabs and chunks. The glowing tooth-shaped cobalt pendant dims!

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

n.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

npcs.name: Broken statue
npcs.description: than chips of stone now.  He drags himself around with an errie scraping noise, but is otherwhise completely silent.

692.name: Rubble Pile
692.description: Filling most of one corner, near one of relatively shallow walls, is a huge pile of stone slabs, columns, and blocks, all flawed in one way or another. Compared to the size of the quarry, and its depth in some of its farther reaches, you can tell that whoever harvested the stone was skilled and left little waste. Dark iron streaks run thick through the rock.

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

693.name: Quarry Bottom
693.description: Rainwater has collected into a large clear pond here in the deepest recesses of the quarry, where the walls are some twenty to thirty feet high. Fish dart about in the water, and there is little debris littering the area. A small stone chair perched near the edge of the pool tells you this is no longer a place of work, but perhaps of recreation.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

go into.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

695.name: Fenced Path
695.description: Stone pillars, each about two feet high, are driven into the ground along each side of the path at steady intervals. Long thin columns cap the fence posts, breaking only where the path curves too much to accommodate a continuous stone rail.

nw.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

696.name: Before a Low Stone House
696.description: A small dwelling has been expertly crafted from seamless slabs of white granite. Windows and doors are all beautifully carved into the stone, complete with sills, hinges and shutters. Paving stones cover the yard, radiating out from the center in tight circles, alternating between dark and light. A path of white stones leads up to the door, while dark stones trace a path to a workshop around back.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

n.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

nw.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

697.name: Workshop
697.description: Four slabs of stone at least thirty feet high form the square walls of this workshop, and another flat stone of equal size forms the roof. Huge windows, filled with clear quartz, are carved into each of the walls to make use of sunlight, and a massive stone chandelier hangs from the ceiling. Chips of rock litter the floor, crunching softly underfoot.

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

npcs.name: Unfinished statue
npcs.description: she is real.  Apparently so does she, as she claws at the stone in a vain

698.name: Home of the Mason
698.description: The inside of the stone building is quite cozy, though the ceilings are a bit lower than usual. A long stone fireplace fills the center of the room, surrounded by skins, pillows and blankets. Little else decorates the area. A grill rests above coals off to one side, and near it on a rack are several cooking utensils. An etched sign hangs above the fireplace.

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

npcs.name: Bragdh O`lugh
npcs.description: strength - despite their small stature - and their love of stonework, especially monoliths and other large standing stones.

699.name: Bedroom
699.description: More pillows are heaped into a messy pile, covered with several soft skins. Another smaller hearth is built into the wall here, as well as a sconce to hold a candle, and a shelf lined with well thumbed books. The smell of poonk is heavy in the air, mixing with smoke from the fire to create a pleasant relaxing feel.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

679.name: Climbing the Hill
679.description: Though the hill is fairly steep, the ground has been solid and grassy, and the going fairly easy up until now. A large rockslide has ripped away much of the topsoil and any plants rooted therein, making the dirt below soft and unstable. However a large slab of rock lay atop it, apparently the result of a newer slide, making the going fairly solid.

u.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

680.name: Overlooking a Coastal Wood
680.description: You stand atop a rise in the terrain, allowing you a clear view of the sparse oceanside forest. The thin band of trees borders a row of rocky hills along the shore, which rise steadily to meet a large cliff farther east. The result is a small beach, ringed first by the cliff and stone bluffs, then again by the foreboding forest of warped trees. Dark iron streaks run thick through the rock. You remove Zephyr's silk hood. You place a white helmet on your head.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

681.name: Coastal Pines
681.description: The trees here are very different from the strong, tall pines found in the interior. A few stand proud and straight above their brothers, but the majority are stunted and short, with branches growing only on the lee sides - a result of the gale force winds that regularly blow in from the raging seas. The path east curves around a twisted, burl covered tree, while traveling west takes you inland.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

682.name: Watering Hole
682.description: A small brook flows into a depression in the ground here, creating a wide and fairly deep pool of fresh water. This place attracts a variety of wildlife, as is evidenced by the multitude of different animal tracks present. Some of which are the harmless variety, just here for an easy place to drink, but others are those of large predators, looking to make a meal of the weak or slow.

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

npcs.name: Timber wolf
npcs.description: man, and well over two hundred pounds of teeth, muscle, and killing fury. The yellow eyes of this once noble beast now glimmer with insanity.

683.name: Grassy Ridge
683.description: This rolling, grass covered ridge slopes slowly downward, towards the ocean not far to the east. There it meets with the wave beaten rocks of the shore, and a curiously large cliff that seems out of place towering over the small stony bluffs. To your west looms the bulk of the mysterious M'chel, and between you and it a series of jagged, broken foothills.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

684.name: Scrub and Stone
684.description: Lush grass farther to the east gives way to rocky scrub as the ridge comes closer to joining the stony foothills of mount M'chel. It begins to rise steeply in the direction of the mountain, while an unkempt path follows it laterally before it descends into one of the many countless vales and valleys created amongst the jumbled hills and ridges.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

n.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

685.name: Loose Rock
685.description: As you traverse the steep ridge, a small avalanche of tumbling debris is sent tumbling down the hill with each step. Small patches of grass and wildflowers cling tentatively to the loose stone on the slope, safe from most grazing animals, but not from your clumsy footing.

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

686.name: Shifting Stone
686.description: Chunks of rock teeter precariously atop a base of much smaller, looser stone. From time to time your step disturbs the bed of one of the larger pieces, sending it crashing down the slope below, accumulating speed, as well as other rocks along the way. Suddenly you are aware of just how easily you could share a similar fate.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

s.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

687.name: Unkempt Path
687.description: Chunks of stone, fallen from the loose slope above, litter the thin footpath while weeds and hardy grasses have taken root in the relatively stable soil. It looks as though nobody really comes this way anymore, or at least if they do, they dont have as much problem with the difficult trail as you. A delicate golden flower opens toward the sky, its petals catching the light.

n.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

w.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

npcs.name: Scrub goat
npcs.description: rock, paying no heed to the loose stone beneath its hooves.

npcs.name: Scrub ram
npcs.description: twice the size of it's head.  How it holds the disproportionately huge headgear aloft is some miracle of nature.

688.name: Old Slide
688.description: The aftermath of an old landslide has taken out much of the path here, which continues some distance away on the other side of the debris field. Trying to reach it from this side would be complete madness, disturbing the loose rock would make another slide inevitable. Curiously, a much more defined path can be seen skirting the slide, straight down the ridge.

e.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

d.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

694.name: Quarry Pond
694.description: Silt and sediment have settled to the bottom of the large circular pit, and perched atop them is a curious pile of huge stones. Aquatic vegetation grows sparsely, and several species of fish, all of which look plump and well fed, swim languidly about the pool. Something about the whole area seems a little too staged to be real, and you cant quite shake the feeling of being watched.

u.to_zone: Foothills of Mt. Mchel - Mt. M'chel Region

npcs.name: Lightning eel
npcs.description: thousand candles, it'd probably be best to stay away from this one.

npcs.name: White koi
npcs.description: little more than - swim, swim, swim, hey, I've got a castle!  Swim, swim,
